name: gudhi_s2_topology_recovery
description: Tests whether entropy-normalized QIT features recover S² topology. K3_bloch is ground truth (Bloch sphere). K2_norm is the main hypothesis (entropy-normalized off-diagonal). Expected: H2=1 for K2_norm and K3_bloch.

pytorch.reason: tensor state construction and partial trace

pyg.reason: attempted but not applicable; primary tools sufficient

sympy.reason: analytic verification of entropy normalization formula

gudhi.reason: Rips filtration for persistent homology H0/H1/H2

hypothesis_tests.conclusion: K3_bloch (Bloch sphere) confirms S2 topology with Alpha complex. K4_full (QIT + Bz) also recovers S2. K2_norm H2 signal is noise (decreasing with density) -- QIT features alone collapse the sphere; Bz is required. Rips max_dim=2 is insufficient for S2 detection -- Alpha complex required.

K1_phi_blind_trivial.description: K1 on 10x10 grid -- phi-blind, Alpha complex should give trivial H2

rips_dim2_fails_S2.description: Rips max_dim=2 on K3_bloch 10x10 -- should FAIL to detect S2

K2_norm_H2_is_noise.description: K2_norm H2 max persistence should decrease with density (noise, not signal)
K2_norm_H2_is_noise.noise_hypothesis: H2 bars in K2_norm are numerical artifacts from grid quantization

polar_collapse_comparison.description: K2_raw vs K2_norm at theta=0,pi only (20 phi values each)
polar_collapse_comparison.note: sin(theta)/2 is ALSO 0 at poles for raw! The distinction is that K2_raw middle-latitude points have DIFFERENT scale than K2_norm middle-latitude points. Run persistence to compare.

alpha_K3_bloch_15x15.description: Alpha complex on K3_bloch 15x15 -- ground truth S2 check

alpha_K4_full_15x15.description: Alpha complex on K4_full 15x15 -- QIT + phase + Bz S2 check

scale_comparison.description: Feature scale comparison K2_raw vs K2_norm on 15x15 grid
